title: Intro
notes: The point of the video is to show how I learn how to do max out all medals on the challenge mode. You'll see my thought process and strategies I use

title: Fuck it; we'll do it live
notes: I'm going to be playing these challenges back to back unedited

title: Note about live reaction
notes: I was sick during this recording

title: Experimenting
notes: I was just experimenting to learn the value of different moves such as the GT and the shock wall

title: Ground Pounds and Score
notes: Ground pounds are the highest contributor to score in the game; but the take enemies off the board for generating further combo points

title: Variation Bonus
notes: Maxing out variation bonus is key to posting high scores

title: Variation maxxing
notes: That's why you can see I'm using all these unnecessary combos on single enemies

title: Variation important
notes: In Arkham Aslyum; the variation bonus contributes to a much higher percentage of your score than in the other games. Especially on maps with relatively small encounters like this one.

title: Variation Falloff
notes: Of course it falls off in later challenges such as Rumble in the Jungle extreme; where you're hitting combos of up to 60+. At that point your ground pounds and takedowns are most valuable

title: Hit While Batclawing
notes: I forgot to account for the fact that this nearest thug would hit me after the animation. He was standing right next to me so of course he was going to hit me.

title: Mental List of Moves
notes: I'm trying to keep a mental list of moves that I've already done so I know which ones I still need to do to get a 9x variation bonus

title: Pipe Hitbox
notes: This definitely looks like it should have cleared after the fact

title: Cleared despite low performance
notes: Despite my lack of ability to avoid attacks; maintain a combo; or even max out my variation bonus; I still manage to clear all three medals for this challenge first try. Demonstrates that these challenges are not unattainable

title: Rebinds
notes: I brought my bindings into parity with my AC bindings. That game lets you bind scroll wheel to batarng/batclaw in the launcher unlike AA. You have to go into the config to add it. The benefits are worthwhile as you can continue to pivot your camera and move while using your quickfire gadgets.

title: Stealth Medals
notes: In order to complete the Predator challenges; we need to takedown enemies with specific moves

title: Explosive Gel Ceiling Spray
notes: Make sure you turn away from the wall and point the camera up to avoid accidentally

timecode: spraying the gel on the ground or the wall

title: Wait for Patrols Sync
notes: We just have to wait for the patrols to sync.

title: Don't need 3 walls
notes: Contrary to my previous understanding; you don't need 3 KOs with 3 walls. You can use two walls; which is the method I use now.

title: Not the whole room is labbed
notes: I don't have the whole challenge labbed out as you'll soon see. Only the setup with the three walls; which is now obsolete

title: Silent Takedown
notes: I was hoping to drop down behind that thug for a silent takedown

title: Whoops
notes: This is what I meant before about aiming upwards. You pretty much have to aim straight up in the air at a 90 degree.

title: Forced to adapt
notes: Now I'm basically forced to adopt a 3-for-2 weak wall setup since I missed my first gel. I wanted to challenge myself not to restart. This is probably where I discovered the setup

title: Revenge!
notes: I'm actually able to execute my ST this time

title: LUCKY
notes: I got lucky in that the thug got launched up here by the explosive gel. Setting me up for a takedown on the thug investigating his body

title: Chain into final medal
notes: I can chain this into the final medal by hitting the guy's buddy with a batarang first

title: Coast Your Way to Victory
notes: This means that you can feel free to mess up quite a bit and still clear 3 medals; as long as you make sure you to perform each move at least once per round.

title: Careless
notes: I wasn't watching this other guy close enough that I knocked back earlier. I don't notice that he's getting up before I've already committed to the GT

title: Won't give up
notes: My first instinct wasn't to restart because I'm trying to demonstrate that it's possible to clear three medals while making mistakes. Still embarassing to be hit in such simple ways though. Trying to give you the authentic experience! I'm clearly going to be short of the goal unless I play exactly perfectly

title: GT Mistake
notes: My mistake there was not scanning the area before committing to the GT

title: Importance of Rhythm
notes: It just demonstrates that even experienced players can make mistakes when knocked off their flow. It can be very difficult to get back into the rhythm of combat if you don't make a conscious effort to get out of the fight; take a second; and start on a new rhythm

title: 8x Variation Still Respectable But..
notes: is still a decent amount but for 1 extra move it increase it by almost 50%. It is admittedly hard to budget for moves that insta-KO enemies like GT or SCT though

title: Incorrect Guess
notes: I failed to guess that the high sec henchman would attack first. If I had I would have reacted differently

title: Buffering AR
notes: You have to buffer your aerial redirect at least a half second in advance while you're not stuck in an animation to be able to dodge the high sec

title: Sloppy
notes: That was super sloppy all around

title: Pausing
notes: You can tell I visibly pause here while thinking what to do

title: Baiting attacks
notes: Baiting involves getting an enemy to commit to a position for his attack and then moving away.

title: Stalling
notes: What I mean by stalling is waiting for enemies to reset while I think about what I should do next. It's a good way to avoid dropping your combo if you're out of enemies to hit or can't think of what to do.

title: Crowd Control
notes: I've got a lot of dudes crowd controlled here with throw; strikes; counter; and ultra batclaw. The fewer dudes you have ready to attack you; the more predictable a situation is.

title: Failure to Clear
notes: I committed to a blind GT that I wasn't clear to do

title: Marker 31
notes: I didn't account for the projectile so I got hit and stun locked for a combo with the thug next to me

title: Now Clear for GT
notes: Now I'm clear for GT because I CC'd everyone first

title: Reposition for Batclaw
notes: I reposition so I can hit all three with the ultra batclaw  and also because you have a short window where enemies won't attack you after doing AR. Usually long enough for an AR

title: Batarang Last Dude Doesn't KO
notes: Unlike in the sequels; quick combo batarang doesn't instantly KO the last guy; only if his HP is low enough.

title: Spacing
notes: In general; spacing gives you a huge advantage to pull off risky maneuvers safely.

title: Marker 32
notes: Stalling real quick while I plan my next strategy

title: Batclaw Targetting Error
notes: I got hit because I was expecting the offending thing to be immobilized by the ultra batclaw

title: Batarng into GT Last Guy
notes: I can usually use quick batarang to setup a GT against the last guy unlike in later games

title: I just Learned QB+GT On Last Guy
notes: I just learned that quick batarang doesn't instantly KO the final guy

title: Marker 34
notes: I'm just waiting for a steep enough angle that will actually cause the henchman to be pulled over the railing instead of falling next to it

title: Ledge Takedown
notes: I'm going to go for the ledge takedown on this guy

title: Trailed Off
notes: So I should reposition so I'm not caught off guard

title: Get Trolled
notes: I definitely got trolled by this collision but I manage to recover kinda

title: Marker 35
notes: Saved myself with a cape stun. When in doubt; use it to give yourself a little time to figure out how to react to the situation. Fleeing vs; fighting; vs eliminating. The other enemies in the room and their locations will determine which approach is most appropriate

title: Marker 36
notes: Sometimes thugs will look up at gargoyles when they are alert. If you are on top of one when this happens; they will detect you

title: Railing Corner Takedown
notes: I meant to say on the corner of the railing. Even though it's clearly not a solid surface; it still blocks you from enemy vision

title: Forcing Climb Down
notes: The prompt was only allowing me to climb over the railing

title: Silent GT
notes: In later games; the enemies can hear the noise of GT from across the map and will rush to investigate

title: Single Minded Thugs
notes: Unfortunately; if thugs are busy with another task; like following Joker's orders; they will ignore the sonic batarang lure

title: Batarang Lure
notes: When in doubt; making noise with regular batarang can still function as a lure

title: Challenge
notes: Maybe that's part of the challenge though

title: The Point
notes: While you should spend time rehearsing repeatable sequences of attacks in predator challenges; I wanted to demonstrate that they are doable on the fly

title: CC + GT
notes: This is a good example of crowd controlling with the ultra batclaw and then doing GT on an isolated target

title: Multi-hit
notes: Got a multi-hit there

title: Batarang Against Stunstick
notes: Batarang is always a safe tactic to use against stunstick wielding enemies

title: GT Targetting
notes: Usually the range is more influential than the movement direction your holding when the game decides which target to hit with GT

title: Marker 39
notes: I expected one of the thugs to run into me and fall on the ground for a GT setup and I overcommitted. I should have confirmed the hit first

title: Marker 40
notes: In general; I like to start my combo with a flying kick; executed by holding run and then performing a strike. This gives you an immediate x2 combo; knocking down the target and allowing you to go right into critical strikes. Whereas you would have to build up a x3 combo with three hite first otherwise

title: Good Bait
notes: That was a good bait from throw

title: Takedown High Sec
notes: It's a good idea to just use combo takedown to finish off the high sec guys so they won't cause you problems.

title: No Multi-Hit
notes: In hindsight; I'm surprised this strike didn't hit both henchmen. I even waited before buffering attack and countered instead so I wouldn't drop my combo because I expected the multi-hit

title: Zsasz
notes: He's basically just a reskinned high sec henchman

title: Batclaw Against Stunstick
notes: Make sure you immediately AR if you yank a stunstick guy or you will take damage

title: Momentum Strikes
notes: Sometimes your long range strikes will have enough momentum to send enemies flying. You can use this to your advantage

title: Unexpected Combo
notes: I only just discovered this upon review of the footage; but it seems like you can combo the QB into UB. Not sure why you would want to do this ; maybe for extra damage and CC or just farming variation bonus

title: Cape Stalling
notes: This keeps you from dropping your combo

title: Marker 42
notes: I'm stunning this guy with the batarang because he could detect and even target me before the animation completes

title: Unexpected Benefit
notes: As a knock on; this is a perfect setup for an inverted takedown unexpectedly. I see an opportunity to do it here

title: RCB Positioning
notes: I'm using RCB to position one of these thugs under the gargoyle

title: Gargoyle Stealth
notes: I think as long as you remain still; they won't see you even if they are focusing the gargoyle

title: Multi-Batarang Suppress
notes: I was considering suppressing the other two with multi-batarang but I didn't want to risk hitting the guy under the gargoyle

title: Isolated Target
notes: Now I'm just picking an isolated target to get the last medal on

title: Time Bonus
notes: Since you are incentivized by the challenge to minimize your clear time

title: Marker 45
notes: I did that highly impractical combo for variation reasons

title: Marker 46
notes: If you don't have at least a x3 combo; batarang will not knock down an enemy

title: Marker 47
notes: This is an example of why hit confirming throws are a good idea before committing to a GT

title: Cancel Rodeo
notes: I really do wish you could cancel the titan rodeo move

title: Shameless Self Promo
notes: Well you can always check out my challenge mode expert video

title: GT Prefers Range
notes: GT will usually prefer range and will sometimes override the direction you are inputing. Perhaps I could have tried repositioning first with dodge or AR.

title: Marker 48
notes: The reason I don't start this fight with a flying kick is that both enemies in front; the baton guy and the knife guy are both immune to basic attacks

title: SCT and Throw Don't Have iframes
notes: The SCT doesn't benefit from iframes when it comes to Titan henchmen in general

title: Rodeo is Canceled
notes: I specifically didn't want to do a Titan rodeo. If you are nearby a fallen Titan and you do a strike; the aggressive suck to target will make you rodeo ride even if you don't push in his direction. I wish this applied when they were standing upright

title: BC + AR Setup
notes: This setup guarantees they can be finished with GT; whereas throw or strike could KO them by themselves.

title: Titan AR Bug
notes: This thing haunts me anytime I'm fighting Titan class enemies. I wish I knew what causes it. Could be they can't be AR'd when in certain animations. Even when you dodge perfectly towards their center of mass.

title: Better Setup
notes: A better setup might be; 3 takedown somewhere; lure thug up ladder above grate; ST against thug checking out ladder body. I ended up implementing this later when I went back to perfect these challenges

title: Better Approach
notes: A better approach is to climb up the ladder and sneak under the window

title: Proxy Det Range Overestimated
notes: I obviously overestimated the range on the proxy detonator for the explosive gel. I thought I was covered while I was ripping the vent off the wall. I could have either place the gel differently or used UB to rip the vent off faster to avoid getting hit

title: Ceiling Has Wider AOE
notes: The ceiling weak wall seems to have a wider AOE when detonated with gel than the wall
